Text: I met with William regarding AB 888, the Safe Homes Grant Program. William shared that the bill has passed and is intended to assist low-to-moderate-income homeowners with wildfire mitigation by providing funding for fire-safe roofs and Zone Zero improvements to create ember-resistant homes.  William advised that applications are expected to open in Spring 2026 and that the California Department of Insurance is scheduled to oversee the program. He also requested assistance in sharing information about the program with our community of approximately 600 homes and expressed interest in providing educational materials, brochures, and other resources for residents.  Additionally, William asked whether educational materials could be made available by June 12, 2026, in advance of the community?s quarterly residents? meeting on June 13, 2026. He also inquired about the possibility of having a representative attend a future residents? meeting to provide an informational presentation on AB 888 and answer questions from homeowners.

Location of Event  Meeting Location: Other Other Location: 9701 E Las Tunas Dr, Temple City, CA 91780 Venue: Temple City Hall Community Patio Event Address: 9701 E Las Tunas Dr, Temple City, CA 91780 Event City: Temple City Event State: CA Event ZIP: 91780  Event Details  Brief Description: Legislative Lunch is a community and business engagement program hosted by the Temple City Chamber of Commerce that connects local leaders, businesses, and residents with elected officials and public agencies. The event provides an opportunity for speakers to share updates on legislation, regional initiatives, and issues impacting local communities and small businesses while engaging directly with attendees through discussion and Q&A. What is the goal of the Event?: The goal of the event is to give local business owners and community members the opportunity to hear directly from Ricardo Lara about current insurance challenges, state policies, and resources impacting businesses across California.
